text: In today's video, I will tell you about the layouts and why are they important in Next.js Like this video and write Next.js in the comment section Let's go to the computer screen Let's roll the intro So guys, I will quickly make a new Next.js app here in terminal I will simply write npm create next app I will select the latest and all default options I will put the name of the layout here and select all the default options I will open this folder in VS Code and wait for everything to be installed And after that I will run my server here I will run the server after I finish writing the dev file So as soon as I run the server, I will get a basic next step I will edit the page.js in the app directory And I will write dev.homepage and I am home page And I have put a very basic home page here I hope this app is mine I have started the server I will click here and start the server And I am home page will come here No surprises whatsoever Now let's say I want to see the navbar in all my pages So what I will do is go to layout.js which is a special file in Next.js In this you will get the option to set the title You can set the title here Let's say I am making a website which is a clone of Facebook Facebook connect, I will write here connect with your friends I will select this co-pilot Connect with your friends and word around you And I will write here in the description Facebook is a And here it has suggested a description I will select it here Now look down here What happens is that after html comes the body What are the children inside the body? Now this is the root layout, this is a mandatory layout Which you will see in the layout.js file outside of Next.js What I can do in this is that whatever I put in it It will be reflected in my overall page structure in all the pages What does that mean? Let's say I make a folder here I make a file named component slash navbar.js And this is my navbar And similarly what I do is that I make a component named footer Footer.js and write footer here by doing rafce And I want my navbar and footer to come on both my pages So I will import navbar here And I will import footer here And I will simply do navbar here and footer here And I will include this navbar and footer here So what will happen is that you will see navbar and footer on every page Something like this, as you can see Navbar, footer, I am home page If I make another page, let's say I make an about page And I write rfce here and I write facebook is And let's say I wrote this random thing and I go to slash about So you will see that here I am getting both my navbar and footer So whatever I have to put on all the pages, what I will do is put it in my root layout But sometimes what will happen is that I want to make an admin page Suppose I made an admin section and I want my admin There is an admin folder in it, let's say login slash page.js And I will write admin login here And I write here paragraph and feel free to login as an admin Suppose I write here and after doing this I want that there are some more pages in my admin Suppose I want to make a page in my admin, I want to make it in this admin folder So I will say that my logout page is ok I will keep the page with the name logout, but again I will put it in admin So we have login and logout in admin, suppose we have comments in admin I will write comments here and all the comments will be shown to my admin Now suppose I want that all the pages of admin have a particular navbar show at the top So what I will do is make a file named layout.js, where did I make layout.js? In admin And I will copy this layout.js file and I want that instead of saying title facebook I want to show admin-facebook and I want to show admin page So whenever I will go to admin and login, suppose I go to admin login So first of all you see this, I will remove the import of global.css and do one or two more things Which is that I will remove the root layout and I will keep it as admin layout And obviously I will not return html here, I want that here what should be I want that I should see an admin bar with children, so what I will do is I will write a span and I will write admin navbar here, and now I will refresh it So you will see that in all the admin pages you will see admin navbar And the rest of the navbar was showing, the footer was also showing So I will do one thing, I will go to tailblocks.cc and I will choose a header from here Because I think the header here is better, so I will choose the header here I will also choose the footer here, I will do view code and I will copy it here I will come here, I have added tailwinds and made a few things here So that you get a little feel, but if you come here, which we have made for admin So you will see that these navbars are visible here, but as soon as I go to slash admin slash comments So you will see that here I am also getting admin navbar, so if I want That something like this should be inside my layout, which only comes on a particular set of endpoints So I can make a layout.js file like this, as you have seen, I have made a layout.js file in admin Now suppose I want to group these layouts, now what does it mean to group them Now see I have grouped them as admin, suppose I want to change its name to admin comments And you understand what problem we are solving here, I will name it admin login And I will name it admin logout, now see what I want to do here If I want to go to admin login, then I will have to do something like this admin slash admin comments, then I will go to admin comments If I want to go to admin login, then I will go to admin slash admin login like this But if you want that you don't have to put this slash admin, then you will have to remove it But if you want to group all the admin endpoints in one folder, then what you can do You can do something like this, you can put it in brackets like this So that you can add a file named layout.js inside it And as you can see, I have renamed admin to an admin in parentheses What will happen with this, that it will not consider the admin folder But it will put this layout.js file on all these endpoints, let's test it So if you see here, I will refresh admin slash admin login I will run the server once, my server was actually closed, I needed to restart the VS code Now see here, as soon as I refresh it, it will give 404 error But if I go directly to slash admin login, then you see here my admin login page will run But at the same time, I have also made a folder here and grouped it And this is called group layouts, and in group layouts, I can put a layout.js file Which will set the title of all the pages inside it, as you can see, admin facebook is done If I go to home, then it will be facebook, not admin facebook So in this way we can use group layouts, and you can also put the layout.js file inside Here in admin comments, in admin login, in admin logout If I want the admin logout to be my layout.js file, then what I will do I will copy the admin layout.js file and here I will say logout I will logout here, and you see here, as soon as I logout here, I will go to logout So in this, let me see, admin logout was the name of the endpoint, admin logout So as soon as I go to admin logout, you will see that in this, let me see what's wrong Default export is not a react component, ok, so I have not actually made a logout here Now you can see here that admin navbar came, logout navbar also came Why did this happen? This happened because first of all, the layout outside will be applied Then after this, because we have given a layout in admin, it will be applied Then we have also given a layout inside it, which is given in admin logout, that will also be applied So what will happen is that it will start populating from here, the layout inside the app It will populate in that, it will say ok, put html, put body, put navbar Put footer, body and html, and what to put in children, we will know later Then we came to this admin folder, we put this layout.js, we put this whole thing instead of children Then after that, it also has children, so we went inside it, where did we go? In this logout folder, in admin logout, there we got another layout.js file And here we put all that instead of children, so that's why logout navbar will also come And the rest of its children will also come, and here you can see admin navbar came first Then logout navbar came, then our page logout came, so this way our nested layouts work And with the help of layout.js file, you can also change the title of any page here So I will write logout here, and I can do it like this, you can see the logout title is done We can also use a generate metadata function, nextjs, so I will show you how you can export this metadata function So in any server component, you can do something like this, you can say export async function generate metadata params And you can generate the title here, so let's say I don't want to make this layout.js file in admin login So I can say directly here, something like this, and I will have to do this outermost here And I can put the title here, let's say I want to put the title login, or I do what it is suggesting me, admin login So if I take you to the slash admin login, then you will see that the title here is admin login facebook So we can use this function, and if I show you the params here, then you see as soon as I reload this page I didn't see anything in the place of params, but if it was a dynamic route, then I would have seen a lot of things in the place of params If it was a dynamic route, then I would have seen the dynamic value of the params in it But now we don't need it, but again we can use this function, write from page.js Because it is a server component, so it will be rendered on the server and set its title I hope you understood how the layout works Again, you will need a lot of practice to understand next.js I am saying that videos are not going to be enough, we will do projects And I have not done a project of react yet, because I want you to stay in next.js and make projects Because next.js is react, it is just file-based routing and there are some good features that make your complex apps easy So if you have not accessed the playlist yet, then do access it Thank you so much guys for watching this video, and I will see you next time Outro Music
